channel.title: theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: A crisis in the making: Can America afford the elderly?
item.description: An increasing number of Americans are living well beyond the average life expectancy. It presents new and complex challenges about how to care for a v...

item.title: Younger men experience more harms using steroids than older men, study finds
item.description: Younger men who use anabolic-androgenic steroids (AAS) experience a greater overall burden and complexity of harms than older men, new Griffith Univer...

item.title: Admissions for child maltreatment decreased during first phase of COVID-19 pandemic, but ICU admissions increased later
item.description: Hospital admissions for maltreatment of children under the age of 2 years declined 31% during the 16-week lockdown at the start of the COVID-19 pandem...

item.title: Researchers identify key mechanism driving progression of fatty liver disease to cancer
item.description: According to recent reports from the Korean government, the incidence of alcoholic liver disease—previously the leading indication for liver transplan...

item.title: 'Hidden' costs of social care after a hip fracture exceed £1.25 billion a year in the UK
item.description: Hip fracture is the most common serious injury in older adults, affecting more than 80,000 people in the U.K. every year. The loss of mobility, functi...

item.title: RHR: Vitamin D: Why a Personalized Approach Is Best
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris Kresser explains why vitamin D supplementation should never be one-size-fits-all. Drawing on new res...

item.title: RHR: Cold Exposure: Why Sustained Beats Extreme
item.description: Cold plunges and ice baths dominate social media, but do they really deliver the best metabolic benefits? In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, ...

item.title: RHR: Why Your Sleep Schedule Matters As Much As Duration

item.title: RHR: Menopause Brain Fog—What’s Really Happening (and How To Fix It)
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris examines research confirming that menopause-related brain fog is real, measurable, and reversible. H...

item.title: RHR: Seed Oils and Colon Cancer – What the New Research Really Shows
item.description: Seed oils have become one of the most controversial topics in nutrition, especially after a 2024 University of South Florida study linked high omega-6...
